name: Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop
name_original: Acueducto romano del Paraíso y Sifón de Burete — circular desde Cahegín
description: Cycle the Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop, a moderate 18.5-mile route through Murcia's diverse landscapes and historic sites.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cehegín offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by rocky, mountainous terrain to the south with peaks like Sierra de Lavia and Sierra de Burete, and gentler elevations to the north and east. The region is framed by the Argos and Quípar rivers, with the latter designated a Site of Community Interest. A significant feature for cyclists is the Vía Verde del Noroeste, a converted railway line providing a peaceful cycling experience through orchards, ancient irrigation channels, and forests. Best touring cycling routes around Cehegín The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop, an 18.5 miles (29.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the Vía Verde, passing through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bike loop from Cehegín, a moderate 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This route explores the local area around Cehegín, offering views of the surrounding plains and hills. Local touring cyclists also love the Greenway of the Northwest – Cehegín Old Town Monuments loop from Cehegín, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) trail leading through the Vía Verde and Cehegín's historic old quarter, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Cehegín is defined by its mountainous terrain, the Vía Verde del Noroeste, and river valleys. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Cehegín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cehegín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cehegín?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Cehegín, offering diverse experiences for all skill levels. These include easy, moderate, and difficult options, with 12 routes specifically categorized as difficult for those seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Cehegín?
A: Cehegín's touring cycling routes feature varied terrain. You'll find rocky, mountainous landscapes to the south, including peaks like Sierra de Lavia and Sierra de Burete, offering significant elevation gains. To the north and east, there are gentler mountainous elevations. The region is also framed by the Argos and Quípar rivers, and a significant portion of routes utilize the Vía Verde del Noroeste, a converted railway line providing a peaceful, often asphalted, cycling experience through orchards and forests.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Cehegín?
A: Yes, Cehegín offers easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. The Vía Verde del Noroeste is particularly suitable, as it's a converted railway line with a gentle gradient and an asphalted track. While specific easy routes aren't detailed here, the Vía Verde itself provides a very accessible and enjoyable experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling trails in Cehegín?
A: The Vía Verde del Noroeste, a major cycling asset in Cehegín, generally allows dogs on a leash. For other trails, especially within natural parks or protected areas like the Ecological Park of Cehegín, it's advisable to check specific regulations regarding dogs to ensure a pleasant and compliant outing.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see while cycling in Cehegín?
A: While cycling around Cehegín, you can encounter several natural attractions. The region is characterized by mountain ranges like Sierra de Lavia and Sierra de Burete, and the Argos and Quípar rivers. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Usero&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes del Marqués&lt;/a&gt;. The Ecological Park of Cehegín, situated between the Argos and Quípar rivers, is also open to cyclists and offers opportunities to observe diverse wildlife and plant life.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of natural beauty and historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Northwest – Cehegín Old Town Monuments loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example. This route leads through the Vía Verde, blending natural landscapes with the historic old quarter of Cehegín, which was voted 'Rural Wonder of Spain 2019' for its charm and heritage. You can also explore nearby historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templar Tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cehegín?
A: Cehegín is an excellent destination for outdoor activities, including touring cycling, due to its generally favorable climate. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer. These seasons also showcase the region's diverse plant life at its best.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016678838/via-verde-del-noroeste-and-carrascalejo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.5-mile (29.7 km) moderate route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40308584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.2-mile (16.4 km) moderate path exploring the local area.

Q: How can I access the Vía Verde del Noroeste by public transport with my bike?
A: Public transport options to Cehegín, particularly the MUR-025-1 bus, may allow bikes, but it's crucial to confirm their specific policy on bicycle transport in advance. For the Vía Verde del Noroeste, you can often find access points in towns along its route, including Cehegín, making it convenient to start your ride.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Cehegín?
A: Parking is generally available in Cehegín, especially near access points for the Vía Verde del Noroeste and other popular trailheads. When planning your route, look for designated parking areas within the town or at the start of specific trails to ensure convenient access.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Cehegín offers challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists, often featuring significant elevation gains due to the region's mountainous terrain. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Northwest – Sifón de Burete loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40343699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Viaduct – Greenway of the Northwest loop from Cehegín&lt;/a&gt;, both categorized as difficult and offering substantial climbs and distances.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Cehegín?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cehegín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountainous terrain, and the peaceful experience offered by the Vía Verde del Noroeste.

Q: Can I find accommodation or cafes suitable for cyclists in Cehegín?
A: Cehegín, being a destination for outdoor activities, offers various amenities for visitors. You can find cafes and restaurants within the town, and there are likely accommodations that cater to cyclists, especially given the popularity of the Vía Verde del Noroeste and the Camino de Levante pilgrimage route that passes through.
tips: The old railway station has been recovered as a restaurant. It is the ideal place to make a stop on the road.
Located near the town of Cehegín, on the Rambla del Paraíso, we find this curious aqueduct, a work of modern times, built in all probability on Roman remains.

If we arrive from Cahegín to our left we will find the Siphon of Burete. Located on the periphery of the Burete mountains, we are surprised by two pools with their small waterfalls of fresh water where we can cool off on the hottest days. El Sifón de Burete is beautiful and worth adding to our route if we are in the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop?
faqs.answer: The Vía Verde del Noroeste is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. During these seasons, you'll avoid the intense summer heat and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months, making for a more comfortable ride through the varied landscapes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route primarily follows the Vía Verde del Noroeste, which features a compacted gravel track. You'll encounter diverse surfaces, including sections of asphalt and some sandy areas. Its railway origins mean the gradient is generally gentle, avoiding steep climbs, making it suitable for touring bicycles, hybrids, or mountain bikes.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde del Noroeste is generally considered easy to medium difficulty for touring cyclists. Its gentle gradient, following an old railway line, makes it accessible for those new to cycling or looking for a relaxed ride. The route's moderate difficulty grade also confirms its suitability for a wide range of abilities.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several interesting points. You'll pass by the historic Paraíso Aqueduct and the impressive Sifón de Burete. Near Cehegín, you can also find the Cehegín Station – Restaurant and Gelateria on the Northwest Greenway, which is a great spot for a break.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde del Noroeste is a public path, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through towns or near agricultural areas. Be mindful of other cyclists and pedestrians, and ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to cycle the Vía Verde del Noroeste and its associated loops. It is a public greenway, freely accessible to cyclists and hikers.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde del Noroeste and Carrascalejo Loop?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available in the towns along the Vía Verde del Noroeste, such as Cehegín. Many of the old railway stations, now converted into albergues or service points, often have parking facilities nearby, providing convenient access to the trail.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde del Noroeste is well-equipped with amenities. You'll find restaurants and cafes in the towns it passes through. Specifically, the Cehegín Station – Restaurant and Gelateria on the Northwest Greenway is a convenient stop directly on the route for food and refreshments.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino del Apóstol, Transmurciana 16, and, of course, the Vía Verde del Noroeste itself. It also briefly intersects with the Variante Asomadilla - Carrascalejo and Ruta Ciclosegura - Itinerario 6.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the compacted gravel, asphalt, and occasional sandy sections, a touring bicycle, hybrid bike, or mountain bike is ideal for this route. Road bikes with very narrow tires might find the gravel sections less comfortable.

faqs.question: Are there accommodations available near the trail for multi-day trips?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde del Noroeste boasts a supportive infrastructure for cyclists. Many renovated old railway stations along the greenway serve as "albergues" (hostels), offering accommodation, food, and tourist information. You'll find options in the towns the trail passes through.

waypoints.name: Cehegín Station – Restaurant and Gelateria on the Northwest Greenway
